mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, mechanically boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances indicate synchronized demand-supply mismatch, forcing equity risk premiums higher and reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of resilient aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative S&P 500 returns in > 55% of observations, or the forward return differential between rising and falling HOUST regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 315.0, persistent inflation pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll expansion months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real GDP growth and a stable federal funds rate indicates a healthy demand-driven expansion without monetary headwinds, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 102.0, manufacturing sector weakness signals broader economic deceleration, triggering risk-off positioning and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
